summary: Crystal‑clear Miller soundboard captures a superb spring ’73 Boston Garden show highlighted by an epic Here Comes Sunshine and a 16‑minute Eyes of the World.
review: The April 2, 1973 Boston Garden performance is praised for its soaring jams – especially the monster Here Comes Sunshine, the transcendent Eyes of the World, and a fiery Playing in the Band.  The Miller SBD source is described as crisp, well‑balanced and “A+” by multiple listeners, making the recording as vivid today as it was back then.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews overwhelmingly rate the show 4–5 stars and repeatedly praise both the musical performance (standout jams, tight playing) and the soundboard quality (crisp, clean Miller remaster). Only a handful of minor complaints appear.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Miller SBD remaster is called crisp, well‑balanced and “A+” by many; listeners note occasional tiny file glitches but no major drop‑outs or muffling.

standout_songs: Weather Report Suite > Eyes of the World
show_quality.setlist_flow: Set 1 is described as solid with a few longer, less adventurous cuts, while Set 2 erupts into extended jams – HCS opens the second set and flows into Playin' and the legendary Eyes of the World, delivering one of the strongest second‑sets of the spring ’73 tour.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly lauded for “excellent” tone and improvisational fire on HCS, Eyes, Greatest Story and other jams.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned briefly (talking about electric popcorn) but no specific praise/criticism of his bass work.
